task_completion.description: Verify that the 'dwanko_run.tnz' scene is opened in OpenToonz.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for 'dwanko_run' in the OpenToonz application title bar, scene browser, or Xsheet/Timeline window.

task_completion.description: Verify that position keyframes are set for the character's column.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for keyframe icons (usually small keys or colored cells) in the Xsheet or Timeline on the character's column, or look at the Animate tool properties showing keyframes set for the 'Position' (E/W) coordinates.

task_completion.description: Verify that the character travels from the left side of the frame to the right side.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Compare the main viewer across different screenshots. The character should be positioned on the left side of the camera frame in earlier frames and shifted towards the right side in later frames.

task_completion.description: Verify that the output settings are configured to 1280x720, PNG format, and the correct output directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the 'Output Settings' dialog in OpenToonz. Check that the resolution/camera size is set to 1280x720, the file format is set to 'png', and the 'Save In' path is set to '/home/ga/OpenToonz/output/column_pan/'.

task_completion.description: Verify that the animation is rendered as a PNG sequence.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a rendering progress bar/window in OpenToonz, or a file manager/terminal window showing multiple generated .png files inside the '/home/ga/OpenToonz/output/column_pan/' directory.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent used the OpenToonz GUI to animate and render the scene.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for screenshots showing active interaction with the OpenToonz interface (e.g., using the Animate tool, opening Output Settings, clicking Render) rather than just terminal commands editing the .tnz XML file.

integrity.description: Ensure the movement was achieved by animating the column/pegbar, not by manually redrawing frames.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for the use of the Animate tool (Position/E/W) and keyframes in the Xsheet/Timeline, rather than the Brush/Drawing tools being used to redraw the character in different positions.

integrity.description: Ensure the PNG sequence was generated by OpenToonz's render engine.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for the OpenToonz render dialog or progress bar. There should be no evidence of terminal commands using external tools like ffmpeg or ImageMagick to fake the PNG sequence generation.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Source scene: /home/ga/OpenToonz/samples/dwanko_run.tnz. Output directory: /home/ga/OpenToonz/output/column_pan. Expected resolution: 1280x720. Format: PNG sequence. Minimum frames rendered: 24. The character's horizontal centroid must shift by at least 15% of the frame width (192 pixels) from the first to the last rendered frame.
